title: hymenocallis lacera
intro: Hymenocallis lacera, commonly known as the shellbark spider lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. As a medicinal herb, Hymenocallis lacera has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant's health benefits include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents present in Hymenocallis lacera include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting relaxation and sleep.

side_effects: Hymenocallis lacera can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting the plant's toxic compounds can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while skin contact may lead to redness, itching, and blistering. Long-term exposure to the plant's sap or extracts can also cause liver and kidney damage, as well as increased risk of infections.
